0.content:
The Final Killing Game is a mutual killing game inflicted upon the senior leadership of the Future Foundation, the global organization sworn to eradicate despair. The participants are summoned to a critical emergency meeting inside a Future Foundation off-shore facility. As the assembly begins, an unknown assailant releases a sleeping gas through the ventilation, and every leader in the room loses consciousness mid-meeting. They wake one by one in a sealed underground complex, a heavy steel monitor bangle clamped around each of their right wrists, with no memory of how they were moved.

A controlled Monokuma appears on the monitors to declare the rules of the "Final Killing Game." Unlike the school-bound games of the past, there are no class trials and no blackened-graduates rule. Instead, each participant has been assigned a single secret "Forbidden Action" (NG Action) known only to themselves, and a hidden "Attacker" walks among them, murdering during enforced periods of unconsciousness. The participants are told that the killer is one of their own, and that the game will not end until the Attacker is exposed or only a handful of survivors remain. Trust collapses almost immediately, as the very people meant to lead humanity out of despair are turned against one another. The game is explicitly framed as a final spectacle: the death of hope, staged using hope's own champions.

1.content:
The Final Killing Game takes place inside a sealed, windowless Future Foundation facility, an isolated off-shore installation built into rock and steel. Every exterior route is shuttered, the elevators are disabled, and the only communication is through wall-mounted monitors controlled by Monokuma. Participants cannot leave, and there is no day-night cycle except the one Monokuma announces.

Meeting Room (Central Hub):
- The circular assembly room where the leadership gathered and was gassed unconscious; the participants wake here. A large central table, raised seating, and a wall of monitors.

Main Corridors:
- Long interconnecting hallways linking every wing; lit by cold overhead strips, lined with surveillance cameras and monitors. Several side passages are sealed by heavy bulkhead doors.

Monitor Rooms / Control Stations:
- Rooms housing banks of screens and observation equipment. Monokuma's image and the facility cameras feed through here.

Lounges & Rest Rooms:
- Plain rooms with couches and beds where participants are forced to sleep when the chime sounds; no locks offer true safety during the blackout.

Storage & Utility Areas:
- Supply rooms, a kitchen and dining area, washrooms, ventilation access, and a power/mechanical room.

The Lower Levels (Sealed):
- Beneath the main floor lie maintenance shafts and a hidden control center from which the game is administered. These are inaccessible to ordinary participants and are the domain of the Mastermind.

2.content:
The status of the participants is as follows:
[Fill with your characters]

3.content:
Every participant in the Final Killing Game wears a monitor bangle, a heavy steel bracelet locked around the wrist that cannot be removed by any ordinary means. Each bangle displays a digital clock counting down toward the next forced sleep, and serves as the device through which the game is enforced.

The bangle continuously surveils its wearer for that wearer's personal Forbidden Action. The instant the bangle detects its owner committing their Forbidden Action, it administers a lethal dose of sedative directly into the bloodstream. The victim is rendered unconscious within seconds and dies shortly after, the same agent used to gas the leadership into captivity, now weaponized at fatal concentration. There is no warning chime and no second chance; the punishment is silent, automatic, and absolute.

The bangles also broadcast the periodic sleep signal that forces all participants into unconsciousness on schedule. Because each Forbidden Action is secret and the bangle alone knows it, the bracelet is both a leash and an executioner, ensuring that even careful, peaceful behavior can become a death sentence the moment a participant unknowingly crosses their hidden line.

4.content:
At the start of the Final Killing Game, every participant is privately informed of a single "Forbidden Action," also called an NG Action, assigned to them and them alone. The instruction is delivered secretly to each person so that no one knows the Forbidden Actions of the others, and revealing one's own is dangerous, since it hands rivals a weapon.

A Forbidden Action is an ordinary behavior turned into a death trap. The monitor bangle detects the moment its wearer performs their assigned action and immediately injects the lethal sedative. The actions are deliberately varied and insidious, designed so that some are nearly impossible to avoid under stress. Canonical examples of the kinds of Forbidden Actions assigned include:
- Walking more than a set number of steps.
- Speaking with other people / holding a conversation.
- Being touched by another person.
- Sitting down.
- Looking at a monitor screen.
- Being seen crying, laughing, or showing a particular emotion.
- Going without something, or doing a routine act a particular way.

Because the actions are hidden and mundane, paranoia spreads instantly: any innocent gesture, a tap on the shoulder, a question, a step too far, might be someone's death. The mechanic transforms cooperation itself into a hazard and is the engine that turns trusted allies into suspected murderers.

5.content:
The Final Killing Game runs on a cycle of enforced unconsciousness. At intervals dictated by Monokuma, a chime sounds throughout the facility and every monitor bangle releases a sleep agent, forcing all participants into a deep, simultaneous blackout. No one can resist or stay awake; the entire group falls asleep wherever they are, helpless.

During each blackout, a hidden killer known as the "Attacker" is set loose. The Attacker is one of the participants, but their consciousness is being overridden, so that they move and kill while everyone, possibly including themselves, is supposed to be unconscious. When the participants wake, they discover that one or more of their number has been murdered during the sleep, with no witnesses and no memory.

Because the murders happen in the dark, during a sleep no one chooses, suspicion falls on everyone equally. There is no class trial to resolve guilt; instead the survivors must deduce the Attacker's identity through investigation and inference between blackouts. The game is declared over only when the Attacker is correctly identified, or when so few participants remain that the killing has effectively run its course. The forced-sleep mechanic guarantees that the game keeps grinding forward regardless of how cautiously the participants behave while awake.

6.content:
The Future Foundation is a vast, well-funded global organization founded in the aftermath of the Tragedy to oppose despair and rebuild the world. Largely composed of former Hope's Peak Academy graduates and their allies, it operates internationally with enormous resources, private military capability, and a clandestine command structure that effectively functions as a shadow authority in a collapsed world.

The Foundation is organized into numbered Divisions, each responsible for a different sphere of the reconstruction effort and each commanded by a Branch Head who sits among the senior leadership. The Divisions cover matters such as intelligence and information, defense and security, public order, resources and logistics, public relations, and the rehabilitation of those touched by despair. The Branch Heads convene at high-level meetings to set policy, and it is precisely such a gathering that the Mastermind targets for the Final Killing Game.

The Foundation's stated mission is the total eradication of Ultimate Despair and the restoration of hope to humanity. In practice its leaders are divided over method, ranging from militant zero-tolerance crusaders willing to execute anyone tainted by despair, to those who believe people can be saved and rehabilitated. These internal fault lines, hardened by exhaustion and grief, make the leadership tragically easy to turn against itself once the killing begins.

7.content:
The rules of the Final Killing Game are delivered by the controlled Monokuma and differ entirely from the school killing games of the past:

Rule #1: All participants must remain within the sealed facility. There is no exit and no escape; the doors are locked from the outside.
Rule #2: Each participant wears a monitor bangle that may not be removed, damaged, or tampered with under any circumstances.
Rule #3: Each participant has been secretly assigned one personal "Forbidden Action" (NG Action), known only to that participant.
Rule #4: If a participant performs their own Forbidden Action, their bangle will immediately inject a lethal sedative, killing them. This is automatic and absolute.
Rule #5: Participants are not told one another's Forbidden Actions. Revealing your own is your own affair, and your own risk.
Rule #6: At intervals, a chime will sound and the bangles will force all participants into a deep, mandatory sleep. No one may resist or remain awake during this time.
Rule #7: During each forced sleep, an "Attacker" hidden among the participants will commit murder. The Attacker's identity is concealed even from themselves.
Rule #8: There are no class trials. Guilt must be determined by the survivors through investigation and reasoning between blackouts.
Rule #9: The game ends when the Attacker is correctly identified, or when only a small number of participants remain alive.
Rule #10: Death by Forbidden Action does not count as the Attacker's work; only the blackout killings advance toward the game's resolution.
Rule #11: Additional rules may be announced by Monokuma at any time as the game demands.

The rules are engineered so that mere survival is impossible without risk: to act is to risk one's Forbidden Action, and to sleep is to risk the Attacker.

8.content:
Monokuma is the self-proclaimed host and mascot of every killing game, a robotic bear split down the middle: one half a cute, rounded white teddy bear, the other half a jagged black demon with a glowing red eye and a vicious grin. He is gleeful, sadistic, mocking, and theatrical, delighting in cruelty while hiding behind childish cuteness, and he insists that violence against him is forbidden.

In the Final Killing Game, Monokuma is not acting on the will of his original creator; he is a remotely controlled puppet, operated by the Mastermind of this particular game to deliver rules, announcements, and taunts. He functions as the public face and rule-enforcer of the proceedings, appearing on the facility's monitors to narrate the killing game and goad the participants toward suspicion and murder. Behind the familiar laughter and mascot antics stands a new hand on the controls, using the icon of despair to stage one final atrocity against the leaders of hope.

9.content:
Hope's Peak Academy was a government-sanctioned elite private high school that scouted and cultivated the most gifted students in the nation, known as Ultimates. Students were not admitted by examination but invited as the established best in their field, and graduation from Hope's Peak was considered a guarantee of success and prestige for life. The Academy's stated purpose was to nurture talent and thereby give hope to the future of the nation.

Beneath its prestige, Hope's Peak harbored a darker apparatus. The school maintained a Reserve Course of ordinary, talentless students who paid enormous tuition to fund the Academy, and it conducted secret human experiments in pursuit of artificial talent and "absolute hope." These hidden programs, and the resentment they bred, made Hope's Peak the cradle of the Tragedy. By the time of the Final Killing Game, the Academy itself lies in ruins, its legacy carried on through the Future Foundation that its graduates built and through the despair that its experiments unleashed.

10.content:
"Ultimate" (in the original Japanese, "Super High School-Level") is the title bestowed by Hope's Peak Academy on a student recognized as the very best in the nation at a particular talent or pursuit. The full title names the specialty, such as Ultimate Affluent Progeny, Ultimate Pop Sensation, Ultimate Soldier, or Ultimate Hope.

An Ultimate is meant to embody peak human achievement, and the system was the foundation of Hope's Peak's mission to inspire the nation. Yet the talent system also produced obsession, envy, and ruthlessness; the gap between the talented elite and the ordinary masses became a deep social wound. Many of the figures in the Future Foundation and in the Final Killing Game are former Ultimates, their extraordinary abilities now turned toward survival, war against despair, or murder.

11.content:
The Tragedy, also called "the Biggest, Most Awful Event in Human History," is the global catastrophe that shattered civilization in the years before the Final Killing Game. It began at Hope's Peak Academy and spread outward into a worldwide cascade of despair, riots, war, and self-destruction that toppled governments, ruined cities, and killed or maddened an immense portion of humanity.

The Tragedy was deliberately engineered. Orchestrated through the manipulation of the Reserve Course and the weaponization of despair as a contagious idea, it turned ordinary people against one another and against themselves on a planetary scale. Its survivors live in a broken, ruined world; the Future Foundation exists precisely to combat the despair the Tragedy unleashed and to prevent its return. The Final Killing Game is, in essence, an attempt to finish what the Tragedy began, by extinguishing hope at its source.

12.comment: Ultimate Despair & the Remnants of Despair
12.content:
Ultimate Despair is the name given to the movement and the people who became the living embodiment of despair, the force responsible for the Tragedy. At its core stood a group of brainwashed former Hope's Peak students who, having been broken and converted, dedicated themselves to spreading despair across the world by any means, treating annihilation and suffering as the only true beauty.

The surviving members of this group are known as the Remnants of Despair. Once gifted Ultimate students, they became fanatical agents of ruin, capable of inspiring others to despair simply by their presence or example. After the height of the Tragedy, the Remnants became the Future Foundation's most wanted enemies. The question of whether the Remnants can ever be redeemed, or must simply be destroyed, is one of the central moral fractures dividing the Foundation's leadership, and despair's lingering influence haunts the Final Killing Game from the shadows.

13.comment: Junko Enoshima's Legacy & the Despair Video
13.content:
Junko Enoshima, the Ultimate Fashionista who secretly styled herself the Ultimate Despair, was the architect of the Tragedy: a genius of analysis and manipulation obsessed with despair as the ultimate sensation. Though she is gone by the time of the Final Killing Game, her legacy is the engine of the broken world.

Her chief weapon was the Despair Video, a piece of media engineered to brainwash anyone who watched it, overwriting their will and converting them into devotees of despair. Through this video and her psychological manipulation, Junko broke a class of model Hope's Peak students into the founding core of Ultimate Despair and ignited the global cascade. Her methods, her brainwashing techniques, and the philosophy of despair she perfected survive her, and they form the template for the atrocity reenacted in the Final Killing Game. To understand the Final Killing Game is to recognize Junko's fingerprints on it, even in her absence.

14.content:
Class 77-B was a class of exceptionally talented Hope's Peak Academy students, a close-knit group of Ultimates whose story is told in the "Side: Despair" timeline. Initially ordinary, hopeful teenagers devoted to one another and to their eccentric homeroom teacher, the class was targeted and corrupted by Junko Enoshima.

Through manipulation and the Despair Video, Junko broke this class and converted them into the founding members of Ultimate Despair, the Remnants of Despair who carried out the Tragedy. Their fall, from a bright, loving class into agents of global ruin, is the human tragedy at the heart of the "Side: Despair" background. By the time of the "Side: Future" Final Killing Game, the surviving Remnants of this class are simultaneously the Future Foundation's deadliest enemies and the subject of its bitterest debate over whether the despairing can be saved.

15.content:
Danganronpa 3 is structured as two parallel narratives that interlock. "Side: Future" is the present-day storyline: the Final Killing Game inflicted upon the Future Foundation's leadership inside the sealed facility, and the desperate struggle of its survivors to identify the Attacker and the Mastermind. "Side: Despair" is the background storyline: a flashback chronicle of Class 77-B's school days at Hope's Peak Academy and their gradual corruption into Ultimate Despair, told to reveal how the Tragedy came to be.

The two sides are designed to be experienced together, with "Side: Despair" supplying the history, motives, and identities that give the events of "Side: Future" their meaning. The contrast is the point: the bright, hopeful past collapsing into despair, set against the grim present where the survivors of that collapse fight one last time over whether hope can endure. The truths hidden in "Side: Despair" are the keys that unlock the mysteries of the Final Killing Game.

16.comment: Hope vs Despair: Munakata vs Naegi
16.content:
The ideological heart of "Side: Future" is the clash between two visions of how to fight despair, embodied by two leaders of the Future Foundation. Kyosuke Munakata, a steel-willed senior commander, believes despair is a contamination that must be cut out without mercy; to him, anyone tainted by despair, even reformed Remnants or those who merely consort with them, is an enemy to be eliminated for the sake of an uncompromising, militant hope. His crusade is absolute, and it makes him willing to kill former allies in the name of a despair-free world.

Opposing him stands Makoto Naegi, a young survivor of an earlier killing game and a true believer in hope as compassion. Naegi insists that people, even those who fell to despair, can be saved and rehabilitated, and he refuses to abandon them. The friction between Munakata's purge and Naegi's faith in redemption defines the moral battlefield of the Final Killing Game, and the Mastermind exploits exactly this rift, weaponizing the leadership's own disagreement to turn the champions of hope into murderers.

17.comment: The Mastermind of the Final Killing Game (Hidden Truth)
17.content:
(Hidden Truth — to be withheld from the surface scenario.)

The Final Killing Game is not the work of Junko Enoshima or a surviving despair fanatic, but of a Mastermind hidden among the participants themselves, someone trusted as a fellow leader of the Future Foundation. The Mastermind operates the controlled Monokuma, assigned every Forbidden Action, and engineered the gassing of the meeting room, all from within the very group sworn to fight despair.

Their motive is the cruelest twist of all: it is born not of despair but of a warped conception of hope. The Mastermind believes that the world's hope has grown corrupt, complacent, or false, and that the only way to forge a true and unshakable hope is to put it through the crucible of a final killing game, to let the leaders of hope murder one another so that whatever survives is hope purified by despair. It is hope used as a justification for atrocity, a mirror image of Junko's despair. The Mastermind's true identity and this hope-driven motive must remain concealed until the climax; revealing it prematurely would collapse the mystery that the survivors are meant to unravel.

18.comment: Izuru Kamukura & the Kamukura Project (Hidden Truth)
18.content:
(Hidden Truth — background secret tied to the deepest layers of the lore.)

Izuru Kamukura is the product of Hope's Peak Academy's most secret and most monstrous experiment, the Kamukura Project (the Hope Cultivation Project). Seeking to artificially create the embodiment of "absolute hope," the Academy took a single subject and surgically and conditioning-wise erased their original identity, implanting in them every Ultimate talent the school had ever cultivated. The result was Izuru Kamukura: a being of overwhelming, all-encompassing ability and a cold, empty affect, possessing every talent yet finding the world numbingly predictable and boring.

The project's hidden costs, the sacrifice of its subject and the resentment of the Reserve Course that funded it, were among the sparks that ignited the Tragedy. Kamukura's existence, his connection to the founding of Ultimate Despair, and the role he plays behind the scenes are among the most carefully guarded truths in the saga. His presence shadows the events of the Final Killing Game, but his nature should be revealed only at the proper moment, as it explains the origin of both the absolute hope and the absolute despair that the game is built to exploit.

19.comment: The Identity of the Attacker (Hidden Truth)
19.content:
(Hidden Truth — the central mystery of the killing game; do not spoil.)

The Attacker who murders during the forced-sleep blackouts is one of the participants, but the horror is that the Attacker does not act of their own conscious will. The Mastermind has rigged the game so that one participant is induced or conditioned to rise during the blackout, in a controlled, sleepwalking-like state, and carry out killings while believing themselves asleep along with everyone else. The Attacker may have no memory of their crimes and may genuinely suspect everyone else, even searching sincerely for the killer who is in fact themselves.

This is what makes the Final Killing Game so insidious: the murderer is not a willing traitor but an unwitting weapon, and exposing them requires the survivors to reason past the assumption that the killer must be acting deliberately. The Attacker's true identity, and the mechanism by which they are made to kill, are the linchpin of the entire mystery. They must be concealed from the surface scenario and revealed only through investigation, lest the game's central question, "who among us is killing while we sleep, and do they even know it?", be robbed of its meaning.
